issues_found: Source body was truncated at 'Do newer hazard models alter physica', preventing verification of the physical risk analysis section and any associated data or conclusions
Topic classification as 'macro catalysts' is loose; this is climate-related methodological research rather than traditional market-moving catalysts like rate decisions or economic data releases
Source is ECB staff blog content rather than official ECB policy communication - the record correctly treats it as commentary but this limits its standing as a formal policy reference
human_review_reason: Source body truncation prevents full verification; physical risk section not captured. Record is generally accurate and well-sourced but topic classification as 'macro catalysts' may overstate market-relevance. Human review needed to assess whether climate methodological research warrants archive inclusion given the specialized, non-market-moving nature.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: The why_it_matters correctly captures the analytical value but could note this represents ECB staff research methodology rather than official policy findings.
